mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0 percent signals severe labor market slack and weak consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer spending recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over 60-day horizons, or retail sales and housing starts diverge without generating predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain negative or flat during industrial production expansions, or the signal loses statistical significance outside recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally during inflation accelerations, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with declining unemployment rates indicate labor market tightening, boosting corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or zero Rank IC, or payroll growth without unemployment decline fails to predict equity direction.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth sustains corporate revenue expansion and earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during expansion phases.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns turn negative during high GDP growth periods, or the signal predictive power vanishes when applied to contractionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 90.0 signals severe manufacturing contraction and supply chain disruption, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the threshold fails to predict short-term drawdowns in walk-forward tests.
